title: Broad Phase, Circle, Rect, Mask, Seam, and Swept Collision Detection · Independent Program Trial
summary: An independent adaptive-arcade team receives a different viewport, device, frame rate, seed, entity count, accessibility profile, platform, and delivery constraint and transfers broad phase, circle, rect, mask, seam, and swept collision detection into a changed layered collision detection pipeline. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of layered collision detection pipeline with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, c…
objectives: Choose circle, AABB, rotated bound, polygon, or mask from acceptable false positives, cost, and player expectation.
Use groups, spatial partitioning, sweep, or bounded pair sets to reduce candidates without dropping possible overlaps.
Compare squared center distance with squared radius sum and test tangent, overlap, separation, zero radius, and float tolerance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Collision Shapes
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 1186 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer broad-phase pruning, circle or rectangle checks, masks where justified, seam cases, and swept tests for tunneling; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Collision Shapes.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose circle, aabb, rotated bound, polygon, or mask from acceptable false positives, cost, and player expectation. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Collision Shapes, keep Collision Shapes connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

options.text: Pixel-perfect collision is always the fairest and best choice. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Collision Shapes.

For commit to the mission model, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Collision Shapes for layered collision detection pipeline.
State the Collision Shapes preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A lab Asteroids team handles case 5279 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer broad-phase pruning, circle or rectangle checks, masks where justified, seam cases, and swept tests for tunneling; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Test segment or swept motion and toroidal images for fast movement and edge crossings.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test segment or swept motion and toroidal images for fast movement and edge crossings. Artifact under review: layered collision detection pipeline.

options.text: The Swept Seam Cases record says layered collision detection pipeline looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the audit the proposed evidence packet evidence boundary for Swept Seam Cases.

For audit the proposed evidence packet, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Swept Seam Cases for layered collision detection pipeline.
The record names the assumption, applies Swept Seam Cases, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Swept Seam Cases
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-swept-seam-cases-ff3sjj" that uses case 1339 to layer broad-phase pruning, circle or rectangle checks, masks where justified, seam cases, and swept tests for tunneling. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Swept Seam Cases.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Swept Seam Cases, keep Swept Seam Cases connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-swept-seam-cases-ff3sjj
# Competency: asteroids-swept-seam-cases.
# Case 1339: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def evidence_asteroids_swept_seam_cases_ff3sjj(distance_squared: float = 400.0, radius_sum: float = 24.0, broad_phase_pairs: int = 18, narrow_phase_pairs: int = 3, swept_tested: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_ff3sjj = "ff3sjj-jjs3ff-1339"
    assert evidence_variant_ff3sjj
    evidence_axes_ff3sjj = (
        "playerff3sjj",
        "seed1339",
        "inputjjs3ff",
        "frameff3sjj1339",
        "state1339ff3sjj",
        "playtestjjs3ff1339",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_ff3sjj)) == 6
    if distance_squared < 0.0 or radius_sum < 0.0:
        return False, distance_squared, "collision-domain"
    if narrow_phase_pairs > broad_phase_pairs:
        return False, narrow_phase_pairs, "phase-inversion"
    overlaps = distance_squared <= radius_sum * radius_sum
    if not swept_tested:
        return False, narrow_phase_pairs, "tunneling-gap"
    if broad_phase_pairs < 1:
        return False, broad_phase_pairs, "missing-broad-phase"
    assert narrow_phase_pairs <= broad_phase_pairs
    return True, int(overlaps), "layered-collision"
